revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:健康ランド]]
{{No references|date=January 2013}} [[Image:Nara-Kenko Land.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Nara Health land ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|qid=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}})]] [[Image:Yuzawa Kenko-Land.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Yuzawa Health land ({{ill|Yuzawa, Niigata|en|Yuzawa, Niigata|qid=Q1204269|lt=Yuzawa Town|1=Yuzawa, Niigata}}), closed in September 2022]] [[Image:Ashikaga Kenkou Land 001.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Ashikaga Health land ({{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|en|Ashikaga, Tochigi|qid=Q374477|lt=Ashikaga City|1=Ashikaga, Tochigi}}), scheduled to close in September 2025]] [[File:みのりの湯.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{ill|Minori no Yu|ja|みのりの湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minori no Yu|1=Minori no Yu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11279230}} exterior ({{ill|Kashiwa|en|Kashiwa|qid=Q319743|lt=Kashiwa City|1=Kashiwa}}), closed in January 2022]]
'''Kenkō Land''' (健康ランド) refers to a large-scale Japanese {{ill|Public bathing|en|Public bathing|qid=Q785952|lt=Public Bathhouse|1=Public bathing}} equipped with various types of tubs such as {{ill|Jacuzzi|ja|ジャグジー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jacuzzi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 風呂|1=Furo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2637646}} baths, medicinal baths, cascade baths, and {{ill|Sauna|en|Sauna|qid=Q57036|lt=Sauna|1=Sauna}}; plus facilities like a {{ill|Massage|en|Massage|qid=Q179415|lt=Massage|1=Massage}} corner, rest areas, {{ill|Karaoke|en|Karaoke|qid=Q229345|lt=Karaoke|1=Karaoke}}, game corners, and dining halls. It is also called a '''Kenkō Center''', a '''{{ill|Kurhaus|de|Kurhaus|ja|クアハウス|fr|Kursaal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurhaus|1=spa house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3200567}}''', or a '''{{ill|Spa|ja|スパ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spa|1=Spa (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q233200}}'''.
== Overview == Under each prefecture’s {{ill|Public Bathhouse Ordinance|ja|公衆浴場条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public Bathhouse Ordinance|1=Public Bathhouse Ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11392191}}, Kenkō Land facilities are classified as “Other Public Bathhouses,” sharing similarities with public {{ill|Sauna|en|Sauna|qid=Q57036|lt=Sauna|1=Sauna}} establishments.
Unlike typical {{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|qid=Q1046648|lt=Sento|1=Sentō}} bathhouses (ordinary public baths), they provide a full set of bathing items such as {{ill|Towel|en|Towel|qid=Q131696|lt=Towel|1=Towel}}, {{ill|Soap|en|Soap|qid=Q34396|lt=Soap|1=Soap}}, {{ill|Shampoo|en|Shampoo|qid=Q180204|lt=Shampoo|1=Shampoo}}, and {{ill|Razor|en|Razor|qid=Q13422881|lt=Razor|1=Razor}}, as well as a rented {{ill|Gown|en|Gown|qid=Q1036729|lt=Gown|1=Gown}} for use inside the facility. Because of this, patrons can visit empty-handed without issue.
Since these facilities are not subject to the {{ill|Price Control Ordinance|ja|物価統制令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Price Control Ordinance|1=Prices Control Ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11570643}}, admission usually costs about 1,000 to 2,500 yen per adult—more expensive than a typical sento (about 400 yen per adult<ref>[https://www.zenyoku.1010.or.jp/fee/ 入浴料金表]（全国公衆浴場業生活衛生同業組合連合会）</ref>). In the 2010s, however, some venues offered a discounted bathing-only option of around 500 yen if guests bring their own towels and do not use the facility’s amenities. Many Kenkō Land sites also charge by the hour (e.g., 2 to 6 hours).
Some facilities pipe in natural {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} water.
Certain establishments operate 24 hours a day<ref group="注">Even if advertised as 24 hours, many require patrons to leave briefly in the morning (around 8–10 AM) for cleaning or to prevent continuous overnight stays, reopening a few hours later—perhaps around noon. Others require customers to settle their bill once 24 hours have elapsed since entering. Additionally, some offer nighttime or early morning discounts.</ref>, offering large shared “resting rooms” sometimes used as sleeping quarters in lieu of a formal {{ill|Accommodation|ja|宿泊施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Accommodation|1=accommodation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q48835818}}. Officially, this is considered “napping while resting,” not overnight lodging. In recent years, to distinguish themselves from the {{ill|Super Sentō|ja|スーパー銭湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Super Sentō|qq=}} format, some Kenkō Land locations have begun including hotel-quality guest rooms on par with a {{ill|Business hotel|ja|ビジネスホテル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Business hotel|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホテル|1=Hotel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q27686}}.
=== History === Large-scale bathing facilities with an element of entertainment, different from traditional {{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|qid=Q1046648|lt=Sento|1=Sentō}} or {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|qid=Q562930|lt=Ryokan|1=Ryokan}} operations, appeared in 1955 with the '''{{ill|Funabashi Health Center|ja|船橋ヘルスセンター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funabashi Health Center|1=Funabashi Health Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11614124}}''' in {{ill|Funabashi|en|Funabashi|qid=Q271417|lt=Funabashi City|1=Funabashi}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}—commonly referred to at the time as a “'''Health Center'''.” They often catered to a middle-aged and older clientele, featuring {{ill|Taishū engeki|en|Taishū engeki|qid=Q7676431|lt=Taishū engeki|1=Taishū engeki}} performances, among other entertainment.
From the 1980s onward, facilities evolved away from the old “health center” style and took on characteristics akin to public saunas, also serving families. These came to be known as “Kenkō Land.”
In 1980, the [https://yunoizumi.com Yunoizumi Kenkō Center] (operated by Mizō Shōji) opened in {{ill|Zama, Kanagawa|en|Zama, Kanagawa|qid=Q145333|lt=Zama|1=Zama, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. Later, in 1985, {{ill|Komaki|en|Komaki|qid=Q389537|lt=Komaki City|1=Komaki}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}} welcomed the “Lucky Kenkō Land (Komaki Kenkō Land)” from {{ill|Yūkō Lucky Group|ja|ユーコーラッキーグループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūkō Lucky Group|1=YUKO LUCKYGROUP INC.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11345861}}—the first facility to carry the actual name “Kenkō Land.” In 2003, it was renovated and renamed “{{ill|Komaki Tennen Onsen Spa Gāra|ja|小牧天然温泉 スパガーラ|lt=Spa Gāra|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaki Tennen Onsen Spa Gāra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},” adopting the “Super Sentō” designation. It later closed on April 30, 2018, due to aging infrastructure and other factors.
In 1989, [https://www.kur-hotel.co.jp/ Kur & Hotel Co., Ltd.] opened “[https://www.kur-hotel.co.jp/isawa/ Isawa Kenkō Land]” with an attached hotel in {{ill|Fuefuki|en|Fuefuki|qid=Q849434|lt=Fuefuki City|1=Fuefuki}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. They expanded in 1995 with “[https://www.kur-hotel.co.jp/shinsyu/ Shinshū Kenkō Land]” in {{ill|Shiojiri, Nagano|en|Shiojiri, Nagano|qid=Q857272|lt=Shiojiri City|1=Shiojiri, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, and in 2002 with “[https://www.kur-hotel.co.jp/suruga/ Suruga Kenkō Land],” which became one of Japan’s largest Kenkō Land facilities and one of the largest hotels in {{ill|Shizuoka (city)|en|Shizuoka (city)|qid=Q174691|lt=Shizuoka City|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=株式会社 クア・アンド・ホテル |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/smartphone/rosei-koy/kigyo-navi/company/company-048.html |website=山梨県 |access-date=2024-10-19 |language=ja |last=山梨県}}</ref>.
== Footnotes ==
=== Notes === <references group="注" />{{Notelist2}} === References === <references />
== See also == {{commonscat|Spas in Japan|スパ（健康ランド含む）}} * {{ill|Public Bathhouse Act|ja|公衆浴場法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public Bathhouse Act|1=Public Bath Houses Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11392194}} * {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} ** {{ill|1-day onsen facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}} - {{ill|Communal bath|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Communal bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Communal hot springs|qq=}} * {{ill|Super Sentō|ja|スーパー銭湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Super Sentō|qq=}} * {{ill|Accommodation|ja|宿泊施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Accommodation|1=accommodation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q48835818}} * {{ill|Taishū engeki|en|Taishū engeki|qid=Q7676431|lt=Taishū engeki|1=Taishū engeki}}
== External links == * [https://www.sauna.or.jp/ 公益社団法人日本サウナ・スパ協会] * [https://www.onyoku-net.org/ 一般社団法人温浴振興協会] * [https://www.j-spa.jp/ 一般社団法人日本スパ協会] * [http://www.n-spa.org/ NPO法人日本スパ振興協会] * [https://www.supersento.com/ スーパー銭湯全国検索]
